Then upgrade your browser to the latest version.)
[Sustainable IT needs a push (7 May 2021). 2 MB](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/abn-amro-verduurzaming-it-heeft-duw-in-de-rug-nodig_tcm16-109386.pdf). To give the market for green IT a boost, we need government intervention as well as more collaboration and knowledge sharing among IT companies across the entire chain. Traditional mindset hinders sustainable IT procurement. Other priorities, costs that are too high and insufficient knowledge are the main reasons for IT decision-makers1 not to opt for sustainable procurement. Interviews with IT companies that present themselves as sustainable — for example because they programme in an energy-efficient way or supply circular hardware — also show that many IT purchasers have a traditional mindset.

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0012
text: To give the market for green IT a boost, we need government intervention as well as more collaboration and knowledge sharing among IT companies across the entire chain. Traditional mindset hinders sustainable IT procurement. Other priorities, costs that are too high and insufficient knowledge are the main reasons for IT decision-makers1 not to opt for sustainable procurement. Interviews with IT companies that present themselves as sustainable — for example because they programme in an energy-efficient way or supply circular hardware — also show that many IT purchasers have a traditional mindset. Decisions are based on a narrow set of key performance indicators (KPIs) that emphasise, for example, speed and continuity, while sustainability does not yet play a role.

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0015
text:
Interviews with IT companies that present themselves as sustainable — for example because they programme in an energy-efficient way or supply circular hardware — also show that many IT purchasers have a traditional mindset. Decisions are based on a narrow set of key performance indicators (KPIs) that emphasise, for example, speed and continuity, while sustainability does not yet play a role. Better one server too many than too little capacity later is still too often the thinking, according to Patrick de Ridder of Cobra Systems. “For the IT professional, safety and reliability come first. Building in overcapacity is therefore commonplace. ‘Do we need ten servers? Then make it twelve.’”
Growing environmental burden from IT forces action.

Then make it twelve.’”
Growing environmental burden from IT forces action. IT contributes greatly to our society and will only grow in the coming years. That means we can keep benefiting from what digitalisation offers us, from working from home to smart buildings and precision agriculture. But there is also a flipside: digitalisation places a heavy burden on the environment, in terms of both energy and raw material use. For example, the production of IT hardware depends on various critical raw materials, while only a small percentage of those materials is recovered after the device is discarded.

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0020
text: IT contributes greatly to our society and will only grow in the coming years. That means we can keep benefiting from what digitalisation offers us, from working from home to smart buildings and precision agriculture. But there is also a flipside: digitalisation places a heavy burden on the environment, in terms of both energy and raw material use. For example, the production of IT hardware depends on various critical raw materials, while only a small percentage of those materials is recovered after the device is discarded. Data centres also place an ever greater strain on our energy supply.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0020
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0021
text:
That means we can keep benefiting from what digitalisation offers us, from working from home to smart buildings and precision agriculture. But there is also a flipside: digitalisation places a heavy burden on the environment, in terms of both energy and raw material use. For example, the production of IT hardware depends on various critical raw materials, while only a small percentage of those materials is recovered after the device is discarded. Data centres also place an ever greater strain on our energy supply. Production and use of IT currently account for 2.1 to 3.9 per cent of global greenhouse gas emissions.2 Researchers estimate that this share will be between 6 and 14 per cent in 2040.3
Expectations of IT parties unevenly distributed.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0021
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0022
text:
But there is also a flipside: digitalisation places a heavy burden on the environment, in terms of both energy and raw material use. For example, the production of IT hardware depends on various critical raw materials, while only a small percentage of those materials is recovered after the device is discarded. Data centres also place an ever greater strain on our energy supply. Production and use of IT currently account for 2.1 to 3.9 per cent of global greenhouse gas emissions.2 Researchers estimate that this share will be between 6 and 14 per cent in 2040.3
Expectations of IT parties unevenly distributed. These facts, combined with increasing pressure from politics and society, call for continued effort to limit the ecological footprint of digitalisation.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0022

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0023
text:
For example, the production of IT hardware depends on various critical raw materials, while only a small percentage of those materials is recovered after the device is discarded. Data centres also place an ever greater strain on our energy supply. Production and use of IT currently account for 2.1 to 3.9 per cent of global greenhouse gas emissions.2 Researchers estimate that this share will be between 6 and 14 per cent in 2040.3
Expectations of IT parties unevenly distributed. These facts, combined with increasing pressure from politics and society, call for continued effort to limit the ecological footprint of digitalisation. As part of the Climate Agreement, the Dutch IT sector has therefore stated the ambition to be climate neutral by 2030.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0023
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0024
text:
Data centres also place an ever greater strain on our energy supply. Production and use of IT currently account for 2.1 to 3.9 per cent of global greenhouse gas emissions.2 Researchers estimate that this share will be between 6 and 14 per cent in 2040.3
Expectations of IT parties unevenly distributed. These facts, combined with increasing pressure from politics and society, call for continued effort to limit the ecological footprint of digitalisation. As part of the Climate Agreement, the Dutch IT sector has therefore stated the ambition to be climate neutral by 2030. All parties in the IT chain have a responsibility here: from the companies that provide the physical IT infrastructure and equipment to the developers of digital services.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0024
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0025
text:
Production and use of IT currently account for 2.1 to 3.9 per cent of global greenhouse gas emissions.2 Researchers estimate that this share will be between 6 and 14 per cent in 2040.3
Expectations of IT parties unevenly distributed. These facts, combined with increasing pressure from politics and society, call for continued effort to limit the ecological footprint of digitalisation. As part of the Climate Agreement, the Dutch IT sector has therefore stated the ambition to be climate neutral by 2030. All parties in the IT chain have a responsibility here: from the companies that provide the physical IT infrastructure and equipment to the developers of digital services. However, IT decision-makers expect the most from data centres; almost half of respondents expect to a reasonable to strong degree that data centres take sustainability into account.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0025
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0026
text: These facts, combined with increasing pressure from politics and society, call for continued effort to limit the ecological footprint of digitalisation. As part of the Climate Agreement, the Dutch IT sector has therefore stated the ambition to be climate neutral by 2030. All parties in the IT chain have a responsibility here: from the companies that provide the physical IT infrastructure and equipment to the developers of digital services. However, IT decision-makers expect the most from data centres; almost half of respondents expect to a reasonable to strong degree that data centres take sustainability into account. For cloud and hosting providers this percentage is slightly lower (43.4), followed by suppliers of IT hardware (41.1).

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0026
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0027
text: As part of the Climate Agreement, the Dutch IT sector has therefore stated the ambition to be climate neutral by 2030. All parties in the IT chain have a responsibility here: from the companies that provide the physical IT infrastructure and equipment to the developers of digital services. However, IT decision-makers expect the most from data centres; almost half of respondents expect to a reasonable to strong degree that data centres take sustainability into account. For cloud and hosting providers this percentage is slightly lower (43.4), followed by suppliers of IT hardware (41.1). The lowest expectations on sustainability are for software companies.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0027
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0028
text: All parties in the IT chain have a responsibility here: from the companies that provide the physical IT infrastructure and equipment to the developers of digital services. However, IT decision-makers expect the most from data centres; almost half of respondents expect to a reasonable to strong degree that data centres take sustainability into account. For cloud and hosting providers this percentage is slightly lower (43.4), followed by suppliers of IT hardware (41.1). The lowest expectations on sustainability are for software companies. This is a missed opportunity, because energy-aware software design, among other things, can deliver major gains.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0028
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0029
text: However, IT decision-makers expect the most from data centres; almost half of respondents expect to a reasonable to strong degree that data centres take sustainability into account. For cloud and hosting providers this percentage is slightly lower (43.4), followed by suppliers of IT hardware (41.1). The lowest expectations on sustainability are for software companies. This is a missed opportunity, because energy-aware software design, among other things, can deliver major gains. Fragmentation of the chain hampers sustainability.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0029
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0030
text: For cloud and hosting providers this percentage is slightly lower (43.4), followed by suppliers of IT hardware (41.1). The lowest expectations on sustainability are for software companies. This is a missed opportunity, because energy-aware software design, among other things, can deliver major gains. Fragmentation of the chain hampers sustainability. A barrier for IT companies to (fully) make the switch to sustainable propositions is that demand is still simply insufficient.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0030
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0031
text: The lowest expectations on sustainability are for software companies. This is a missed opportunity, because energy-aware software design, among other things, can deliver major gains. Fragmentation of the chain hampers sustainability. A barrier for IT companies to (fully) make the switch to sustainable propositions is that demand is still simply insufficient. This is the familiar chicken-and-egg problem.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0031
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0032
text: This is a missed opportunity, because energy-aware software design, among other things, can deliver major gains. Fragmentation of the chain hampers sustainability. A barrier for IT companies to (fully) make the switch to sustainable propositions is that demand is still simply insufficient. This is the familiar chicken-and-egg problem. In addition, fragmentation of the IT chain works against them.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0032
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0033
text: Fragmentation of the chain hampers sustainability. A barrier for IT companies to (fully) make the switch to sustainable propositions is that demand is still simply insufficient. This is the familiar chicken-and-egg problem. In addition, fragmentation of the IT chain works against them. The owner of the problem is not always the solver.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0033
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0034
text: A barrier for IT companies to (fully) make the switch to sustainable propositions is that demand is still simply insufficient. This is the familiar chicken-and-egg problem. In addition, fragmentation of the IT chain works against them. The owner of the problem is not always the solver. For example, grid operators carry the problem when power shortages arise, but IT companies must implement the solutions.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0034
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0035
text: This is the familiar chicken-and-egg problem. In addition, fragmentation of the IT chain works against them. The owner of the problem is not always the solver. For example, grid operators carry the problem when power shortages arise, but IT companies must implement the solutions. This can involve, for instance, a commercial data centre that can organise its cooling more efficiently and a hosting company that must smartly configure its servers in that data centre.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0035
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0036
text: In addition, fragmentation of the IT chain works against them. The owner of the problem is not always the solver. For example, grid operators carry the problem when power shortages arise, but IT companies must implement the solutions. This can involve, for instance, a commercial data centre that can organise its cooling more efficiently and a hosting company that must smartly configure its servers in that data centre. Many solutions also come from outside the chain, such as from academia or start-ups.

metadata.chunk_id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0036
metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0037
text: The owner of the problem is not always the solver. For example, grid operators carry the problem when power shortages arise, but IT companies must implement the solutions. This can involve, for instance, a commercial data centre that can organise its cooling more efficiently and a hosting company that must smartly configure its servers in that data centre. Many solutions also come from outside the chain, such as from academia or start-ups. Combination of government intervention and collaboration in the IT chain is crucial.

metadata.source_slug: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig

id: Verduurzaming IT heeft duw in de rug nodig__c5-sentence-window__0038
text: For example, grid operators carry the problem when power shortages arise, but IT companies must implement the solutions. This can involve, for instance, a commercial data centre that can organise its cooling more efficiently and a hosting company that must smartly configure its servers in that data centre. Many solutions also come from outside the chain, such as from academia or start-ups. Combination of government intervention and collaboration in the IT chain is crucial. A bright spot is that IT companies offering sustainable products and services do cautiously see demand for their products increasing, as they told ABN AMRO.

This is all the more true for sustainable IT propositions that also deliver commercial benefits. To give the market for sustainable IT a nudge and secure a permanent place in the digital ecosystem, government intervention is desirable, such as subsidies and regulation. IT companies themselves can help the market by being transparent about their sustainability efforts and joining forces with other chain partners for experiments and knowledge sharing.
